messages.text: Press Ctrl+P to open the Command Palette — connect, run snippets, or jump to settings instantly.
Ctrl+Shift+P opens the Quick Switcher — jump between open sessions by most recently used.
Multi-line paste shows a preview before sending — protecting you from accidental prod commands.
Assign a color to a session to get a color-coded tab and sidebar dot — great for prod vs staging.
Click the Columns icon in the SFTP panel for two-pane mode — drag files between local and remote.
Add notes to a session (rotation dates, topology tips) — hover the sticky-note icon in the sidebar.
Use Broadcast mode to send the same command to multiple sessions simultaneously.
Pin macros to the button bar (max 8) for one-click execution without opening the macro panel.
Set a MAC address on a session to get a Wake-on-LAN button — boot the machine before connecting.
Enable a Highlight Profile (Settings → Highlighting) to color-code log levels in real time.
Click the Share button in a terminal tab to let others watch your session read-only in a browser.
In the Find bar (Ctrl+F) toggle the .* button to switch to regex search in the terminal output.
Set a Session Keepalive interval in Settings to stop AWS and firewalls from dropping idle connections.
Settings → Data → Audit Log shows a timestamped history of every connect, disconnect, and auth failure.
secureSH supports SSH certificate authentication — set auth type to 'Certificate' and pick your cert file.
Enable X11 Forwarding in session settings to run remote GUI apps locally via XQuartz.
Enable Session Logging (Settings → Terminal) to auto-save terminal output to a dated log file.
Mark a session as a Template to use it as a base for new connections without modifying the original.
Settings → Data → Cloud Sync lets you back up your encrypted vault to S3 or a self-hosted server.
Press F1 to see all keyboard shortcuts. Every shortcut is remappable in Settings → Keybindings.
